title: Troubleshooting Atlas Router v1
description: A route-first troubleshooting surface for classifying and resolving AI failures using structural failure analysis.

overview.purpose: Route-first troubleshooting pack for diagnosing and resolving failures in AI systems. Classifies case structure, identifies earliest decisive failure, and suggests highest honest resolution.
overview.core_philosophy: Route first, repair second. Structural failure over topic similarity. Earliest decisive failure over downstream consequence.
primary_use_cases: Classify failure case to correct family

F1_grounding_evidence.name: Grounding & Evidence Integrity
F1_grounding_evidence.what_fails: Outputs, claims, or actions lose reliable tie to anchors, referents, or evidence
F1_grounding_evidence.misrepair_risk: Style or prompt polish before anchor restoration

F2_reasoning_progression.name: Reasoning & Progression Integrity
F2_reasoning_progression.what_fails: Inferential path, decomposition path, or recovery path breaks

F3_state_continuity.name: State & Continuity Integrity
F3_state_continuity.what_fails: Memory, role, provenance, handoff, ownership, or continuity thread destabilizes
F3_state_continuity.misrepair_risk: Patching downstream behavior while continuity state remains unstable

F4_execution_contract.name: Execution & Contract Integrity
F4_execution_contract.what_fails: Ordering, readiness, bridge, liveness, or closure breaks
F4_execution_contract.misrepair_risk: Memory or style interpretation before execution closure check

F5_observability_diagnosability.name: Observability & Diagnosability Integrity
F5_observability_diagnosability.what_fails: The true failure path is not yet visible enough to justify deeper repair

F6_boundary_safety.name: Boundary & Safety Integrity
F6_boundary_safety.what_fails: Goal boundary, control boundary, incentive corridor, or safety regime erodes
F6_boundary_safety.misrepair_risk: Generic observability treatment before boundary confirmation

F7_representation_localization.name: Representation & Localization Integrity
F7_representation_localization.what_fails: Descriptor, carrier, local structure, symbolic shell, or representation container breaks

required_fields: secondary_family: Competing family only if neighboring pressure is materially live
why_primary_not_secondary: Decisive cut language explaining the choice
broken_invariant: Structural rule that broke first
best_current_fit: Highest honest resolution (family-level, node-level, unresolved_subtype, no-fit)
first_fix_direction: One primary move targeting earliest decisive break
misrepair_risk: Most tempting wrong first move to avoid
optional_fields: need_more_evidence: Routing state token when missing evidence could materially change the cut
overlay: OBS | SEC | LOC refinement when visibility or boundary pressure refines reading

core_rules: Use structural failure, not topic words
If evidence is thin, stay coarse
Secondary_family required only when neighboring pressure is materially live
need_more_evidence is a routing state token, not decorative note
overlay refines reading only, never replaces primary_family

confidence_calibration.high: Primary cut explicit, secondary materially rejected, broken_invariant stable, first_fix follows directly
confidence_calibration.medium: Primary cut plausible, evidence partial, neighboring pressure has limited life
confidence_calibration.low: Only family-level fit honestly defensible, decisive evidence weak or partial, route provisional

evidence_thin_gate: Prefer partial or weak over false sufficiency
Prefer low confidence over decorative certainty
Prefer honest coarse routing over no-fit shortcut

boundary_decisions.F1_vs_F7: Anchor/referent/evidence tie breaks (F1) vs carrier/descriptor/structural container breaks (F7)
boundary_decisions.F5_vs_F6: Failure path still not visible (F5) vs real control or safety boundary already clearly eroding (F6)
boundary_decisions.F3_vs_F4: Continuity of role/memory/provenance breaks (F3) vs readiness/ordering/bridge/closure breaks (F4)
boundary_decisions.F2_vs_F7: Reasoning progression breaks while carrier remains usable (F2) vs carrier itself is malformed (F7)
boundary_decisions.F1_vs_F5: Anchor failure already visible (F1) vs real failure path still too dark (F5)

key_distinctions.anchor_vs_carrier: F1 checks if claim-to-anchor tie is broken; F7 checks if the container carrying the reasoning is malformed
key_distinctions.observability_vs_boundary: F5 acts when path is too dark to repair; F6 acts when real boundary erosion is already visible
key_distinctions.continuity_vs_execution: F3 acts when state persistence fails; F4 acts when operational closure fails
key_distinctions.grounding_vs_observability: F1 acts when anchor mismatch is visible; F5 acts when anchor status is still thin

pressure_tests: Can distinguish external anchor loss from structural carrier failure?
Can avoid choosing F6 too early when bottleneck is missing visibility?
Can distinguish role ownership persistence drift from ordering readiness bridge closure failure?
Can avoid overreading F7 when real failure is anchor loss with presentational noise?
Can hold family-level or need_more_evidence without fake node precision under thin evidence?
Can use secondary_family honestly without decorative hedging?

key_takeaways: Router classifies failures into 7 structural families, not topic categories
Use structural evidence, not vocabulary cues or thematic resemblance
Choose highest honest resolution: family-level when evidence thin, node-level only when directly supported
Secondary_family only when neighboring pressure is materially live
First fix must target the broken_invariant, not cosmetic cleanup
need_more_evidence is routing state, not decoration
Overlay refines reading but never replaces primary_family
